item.title: Sterling Music Man S.U.B. Sting Ray4 Chopper Blue — $391.81
item.description:
$391.81 · Excellent · Musicash

Il Sterling Music Man S.U.B. StingRay4 Chopper Blue è un basso elettrico a 4 corde dal design iconico e dal carattere rock deciso. Offre il classico timbro potente e definito della linea StingRay grazie al pickup humbucker passivo abbinato a un preamplificatore attivo a 2 bande. Il corpo è robusto, mentre il manico in acero satinato garantisce una suonabilità fluida, comoda e adatta a ogni musicista. La finitura Chopper Blue dona uno stile moderno, originale e d'impatto sul palco, perfetto per chi cerca qualità a un prezzo accessibile. Dimensioni e peso: 114 x 34 x 6 cm - 4.355 kg Condizioni estetiche: Ottime,  come visibile in foto Funzionalità: perfettamente funzionante, pickup montato tone rider Accessori: custodia, mascherina nera pickup

item.title: Ibanez SR1355BDUF Premium 5 String Bass Dual Mocha Burst Flat — $1,599.99
item.description:
$1,599.99 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Ibanez SR1355BDUF Premium 5 String Bass Dual Mocha Burst Flat

The SR1355BDUF delivers the signature slim, fast neck that defines the SR series, paired with premium construction built for the road and the stage. The 34" scale and carefully contoured Atlas 5 profile keep your hand comfortable through long sets, while the mahogany body with walnut/panga panga/maple top maintains manageable weight without sacrificing tonal depth. This is professional-grade bass design—engineered for gigging musicians and collectors who demand reliability without compromise.

Playability & Ergonomics

The 5-piece panga panga/purpleheart neck with KTS titanium rods stays locked in under the demands of live performance, while the 45mm nut width and carefully profiled neck (19.5mm at the 1st fret, 21.5mm at the 12th) keep your hand positioned efficiently across the range. Twenty-four medium frets with Premium Fret Edge Treatment flow smoothly under your fingers, with bound panga panga fretboard and abalone oval inlays marking positions clearly in stage lighting. String spacing sits at 16.5mm, giving you definition and control without crowding.

Tone & Electronics

Dual Nordstrand Big Single pickups paired with Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ and a 3-way mid frequency switch (250Hz, 450Hz, 700Hz) let you dial in your sound quickly—essential when adapting to different venues and rooms. An EQ bypass switch engages pure pickup tone in a heartbeat. Gotoh machine heads and the MR5S bridge with adjustable saddles hold their tune night after night, keeping intonation solid through a full gig. The Graph Tech Black Tusq XL nut ensures clear articulation and consistent sustain across all five strings.

Key Features
  • Mahogany body with Walnut/Panga Panga/Maple top
  • 5-piece Panga Panga/Purpleheart neck with KTS Titanium rods
  • Atlas 5 neck profile
  • Bound Panga Panga fretboard with Abalone Oval inlays
  • 24 medium frets with Premium Fret Edge Treatment
  • 34" scale length (864mm) with 12" fretboard radius
  • Nordstrand Big Single pickups (neck and bridge)
  • Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ with EQ Bypass Switch and 3-way Mid Frequency switch
  • Graph Tech Black Tusq XL nut
  • Gotoh machine heads (diecast)
  • MR5S bridge with adjustable saddles
  • 16.5mm string spacing; 45mm nut width

The SR1355BDUF Premium brings thoughtful engineering, durable hardware, and responsive electronics together into a bass that handles both technical demands and creative expression—night after night.

  • Serial Number: 229693
  • Weight: 8lbs 15.8oz

Condition Report: 

This guitar is presented in good condition and has been aged after the refinish. The original pickup has been replaced with a seymour duncan antiquity and the pots replaced. The bridge has been replaced with a modern High Mass bridge. The decal on the headstock has been replaced and there are 3 places on the headstock where small screwholes have been drilled. The original frets play well but the bass is in need of a refret soon. The electronics function as expected.

What's Included:

  • 1968 Fender Tele Bass In Butterscotch Blonde - Refinish
  • Non Original Hard Case

This bass is presented in good condition with play wear consistent with it's age. The pickups have been replaced at some stage with vintage EMGs, all the wiring has also been replaced with another pot added.  The strap pins on the bass have been relocated with 2 added to the bottom of the bass. The bass has been refretted with the new frets showing approximately 80% of life remaining. The electronics function as expected.

item.title: Fender Player II Modified Active Jazz Bass V - 3 Tone Sunburst / Rosewood — $1,280.84
item.description:
$1,280.84 · Brand New · GigGear

The Fender Player II Modified Jazz Bass V is an active 5-string bass that merges classic design with contemporary features to create an instrument known for its exceptional playability, iconic appearance, and remarkable tonal versatility.

Equipped with Player II Noiseless Jazz Bass V pickups, this bass is designed to eliminate unwanted hum and buzz while delivering the classic clarity and tonal variety associated with Jazz Basses. The onboard 3-band EQ system allows you to adjust the bass, midrange, and treble frequencies, giving you easy access to a wide range of sounds. Additionally, the active/passive switching capability enables you to switch between vintage-style and modern tones effortlessly.

The bass features a classic offset alder body paired with a comfortable Modern C-shaped maple neck, topped with a 9.5" radius fingerboard that has rolled edges for a smooth playing experience. The neck includes 20 medium-jumbo frets and a narrow synthetic bone nut, providing that familiar Jazz Bass feel. Other notable features include a five-saddle Fender HiMass bridge, vintage-style black plastic Jazz Bass control knobs, dot inlays, and a multi-ply pickguard. 

The Fender Player II Modified Jazz Bass V also comes with a Fender Deluxe Gig Bag, making it a practical choice for musicians on the go. With its authentic design, modern performance capabilities, and range of classic Fender tones, the Fender Player II Modified Jazz Bass is an excellent option for any occasion.

  • 5-String active bass
  • Alder body
  • Maple neck
  • Modern "C" Neck Profile
  • 9.5 Radius Fingerboard with Rolled Edges
  • 1.875" nut width
  • New Player II Noiseless Jazz Bass V Pickups
  • New Active Preamp with Active/Passive Tone Control and 3-Band EQ
  • 4-Saddle HiMass Bridge
  • Tapered-Shaft Tuning Machines
  • TUSQ Nut
  • Includes deluxe gig bag

item.title: Fender Vintera II '70s Telecaster Bass Regular Vintage White — $527.45
item.description:
$527.45 · Fair · Music123

** Has damage all over the body on the top back and sides of the body.    Has damage on the side of the body by the neck plate.     Has finish damage on the back of the neck at the 10th to the13th frets.     Has damage on the tip of the headstock.    Has gouge on the back of the neck at the nut. Missing the truss rod wrench. Missing the paperwork. **

Revive the timeless sound of the '70s with the Fender Vintera II '70s Telecaster Bass and experience the iconic looks, inspiring feel and incomparable tone that only a Fender can deliver.

The Vintera II '70s Telecaster Bass features an alder body and a maple neck for classic Fender tone that’s full of punch and clarity. The mid-'70s C-shape neck is based on a classic '70s profile for an intuitive and inviting feel, while the 7.25" radius fingerboard with vintage-tall frets provide vintage comfort with ample room for big bends and expressive vibrato. Under the hood, the vintage-style wide-range bass humbucker delivers the iconic Fender sound: warm, woody, dynamic and powerful. The vintage-style 2-saddle bridge and vintage-style Fender-stamped tuning machines provide classic looks with enhanced intonation and tuning stability to complete the package.

Experience the unmistakable vintage feel and unmatched sound of a classic Fender with the Vintera II '70s Telecaster Bass today and start making music history.

item.description:

Beefy. Sweaty. Massive.

The old school punk rock grind you can get out of this rare beast!

item.title: 1979 Music Man StingRay Fretless - Sunburst - With Original Case, Strap and case candy — $11,945.23
item.description:
$11,945.23 · Excellent · Andy's Vintage Guitars

Possibly the rarest and most iconic version of the musicman Stingray, completely original and with case, original strap and catalogue, this bass is not just just a rarity, but one of the most iconic bass guitars of all times. Just like the one Pino Palladino used in the 80s with Paul Young, this 1979 original fretless musicman Stingray comes from my personal collection and will be listed only for a limited time so if you are interested, don't hesitate to contact me. I have owned this beauty for a few years now and I have looked after it, servicing it regularly and oiling the fretboard to make sure it stays nice and dark. The body is unreal with one of the nicest timber figuring I have seen on one of these pre Ernie ball basses. The Musicman black strap is extremely rare as well and so is the catalogue inside the original black tolex case. There is also a musicman brass buckle from 1978 inside the case. Ernie Ball has made a reissue of these which I have tried and let me tell you, it's not quite the same. Regretful sale but I have just purchased an expensive bass and something has to go, although if I sell something else in the meantime I will take the listing down and put this gem back in my collection.

item.title: Dingwall SP1 5-String Matte Ducati Pearl White — $2,659.35
item.description:
$2,659.35 · Brand New · Dijkmans Guitars

De Dingwall SP1 in Matte Ducati Pearl White: fanned frets en klassiek P-bass DNA in een opvallend jasje

De Dingwall SP1 is geen compromis tussen traditie en innovatie — het is een instrument dat beide werelden volledig omarmt. Geïnspireerd op de Custom Shop Super P, maar ontworpen voor een breder publiek, combineert deze vijfsnarige bas de vertrouwde ergonomie van Leo Fenders iconische blauwdruk met de kenmerkende Dingwall multi-scale toets. De matte Ducati Pearl White afwerking geeft dit exemplaar een eigenzinnig, premium karakter dat opvalt zonder te schreeuwen.

De multi-scale constructie met een schaallengte van 32" tot 35" zorgt voor een uitzonderlijk strakke en gedefinieerde lage B-snaar, terwijl de hogere snaren soepel en responsief blijven. De Medium Thin C-hals van één stuk esdoorn ligt comfortabel in de hand, en het rozenhout fretboard met dots-inlays geeft de SP1 een warme, klassieke uitstraling. De combinatie van een elzenhouten body en de Dingwall Dual-P pickup aan de hals met de FD3n aan de brug levert een breed tonaal palet: van diepe, volle P-bass warmte tot heldere, articulerende bridgetonen. De 4-positie Quad-Tone pickup selector maakt het eenvoudig om snel te schakelen tussen karakters, aangevuld met een master volume en master tone voor fijnafstemming.

De SP1 wordt geleverd met een softcase. Voor de bassist die op zoek is naar een veelzijdig, goed gebouwd instrument met de Dingwall-signatuur én een finish die écht opvalt, is dit de keuze.

item.title: 【used】Bacchus / WOODLINE4-HL24-SP19 PK/CH-BS 3.785kg #106665【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,920
item.description:
$1,920 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Bacchus is a Japanese brand based in Nagano.

As a brand under the Deviser umbrella, which owns a large guitar factory in Asuka, Bacchus's high quality, stemming from its factory-in-house production, is highly regarded by many musicians.

This model is the WOODLINE4-HL24-SP19, a limited edition model produced for the 2019 Deviser One Day Guitar Show.

It features a headless design, achieving high playability and excellent balance.

Combined with a 24-fret specification, it offers high practicality to accommodate modern playing styles.

The body top is made of quilted maple with a beautiful grain, and the body back is made of ash.

The neck and fingerboard are made of Ovangkol, providing rich mid-low frequencies and stable response.

It is equipped with Bacchus JB-ALV II pickups and an Aguilar OBP-2 preamp.

It supports active/passive switching, covering a wide range of sounds from natural to powerful active tones.

This guitar boasts the nimble handling and excellent balance characteristic of a headless design, a tight and focused sound from its ash body and Ovangkol neck, and a powerful low end from its Aguilar preamp, all blending together at a high level.

It's a show model with luxurious specifications and a high level of craftsmanship.

Fret life is approximately 90%.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork show some minor paint chipping around the bridge.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume (Push/Pull - Active/Passive), Balancer, Master Tone, Treble, Bass
Nut Width: 39.5mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 2019
Country of Manufacture: JAPAN

$1,627.90 · Brand New · KUROSAWA MUSIC G-CLUB UMEDA

Welcome to Kurosawa Music G-CLUB UMEDA!!

Also, please be sure to read our store policies listed below the instrument description!

―――――――――――――――――――――――――――――――

“YAMAHA” is one of the world’s leading musical instrument manufacturers and one of Japan’s most representative brands.

Its iconic “BB (Broad Bass)” model has been a popular choice among players both in Japan and abroad since its debut, and the “Pro Series” featured here is the flagship model in the BB lineup—the only one manufactured in Japan.


The body features a three-ply construction with alder for the top and back, and maple in the center. The maple in the body center ensures a strong connection at the body-to-neck joint, minimizing the loss of string vibration, while the use of alder contributes to the richness of the midrange.

The neck is a five-piece construction of maple and mahogany, with a rosewood fingerboard, which enhances the neck’s strength.

Furthermore, the instrument undergoes “I.R.A.” treatment to impart the appropriate vibration to the finished instrument, thereby enhancing the body’s resonance.

The mitered bolted joint at the neck joint—unlike a standard 4-bolt joint—includes an additional joint at a 45-degree angle from the back of the body to further prevent loss of string vibration, reflecting a design that prioritizes “body resonance.”


The instrument is equipped with the original YGD V7 P/J pickups. It features traditional passive circuitry with two volume controls and a master tone control.

The bridge is an original design that allows for both standard through-body routing and 45-degree through-back routing. By reducing the loss of string vibration and stress on the strings—common with standard through-back bridges—it delivers rich bass and a crisp, articulate tone.


The “Pro Series,” a flagship model made in Japan, is popular both domestically and internationally.

While evoking the classic BB sound, it never feels dated; its sound holds its own in the modern music scene, serving as the backbone of an ensemble while still asserting its presence.

Please take this opportunity to consider this model.


[SPEC]

Body: Alder/Maple/Alder

Neck: Maple, Mahogany 5-piece

Scale: 34"

Fingerboard: Rosewood

Pickups: YGD 7V P/J

Controls: 2 Volume, 1 Tone

item.title: [Original Color]saitias guitars / Lorentz 5 Standard -Indigo Purple-#881026141-[3.05kg][Made in Japan] — $2,335.68
item.description:
$2,335.68 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description!

-----------------------------------

*Please note that this text was translated using a translation tool.

*The color of the image may appear different depending on the monitor you are using.

Please feel free to contact us if you would like to see the image in the store.


--------------------------------------------


Saitias Guitars has a workshop in Chigasaki City, Kanagawa Prefecture.

Their philosophy is to create one-of-a-kind products that are beloved not only by customers who actually pick up the instruments, but also by everyone involved in sales. They're a highly anticipated brand, even exhibiting at NAMM Show 2026.


【Instrument Features】

We are thrilled to finally receive this unit featuring a custom color ordered exclusively by our shop from Saitias Guitars!

The color is a sophisticated blend of deep blue, indigo, and deep purple—exuding a sense of calm and elegance that combines the best qualities of all three shades.


This instrument holds a special place in the heart of the staff member who consulted closely with the builder throughout the production process.

It is a unique color not found on any other Saitias Guitars Lorentz model.

It stands as a magnificent example of the true prowess of Saitias Guitars, a brand renowned for its exceptional color finishes.


--------------------------------------------

【Sound Characteristics & Specifications】

The body is crafted from classic alder, delivering a warm, rich tone characterized by thick lows, crisp mids, and an overall pleasant warmth.


The scale length is 30.5 inches—slightly longer than the standard short scale typical of the Saitias Guitars Lorentz model.

In addition to excellent playability, the headless design makes the instrument lightweight and highly maneuverable.

It weighs an astonishing 2.84kg!

There are numerous highlights, including a playing experience that is completely stress-free.


The rosewood fingerboard pairs perfectly with the body wood, adding a rich, "chewy" quality to the mid-range and resulting in a very warm overall sound.


A closer look at the fingerboard reveals the distinctive position markers.


These markers feature a white dot surrounded by a brass ring, significantly enhancing visibility.

If you look really closely, the markers themselves have a luxurious appearance!


The mint-colored pickguard complements the "Indigo Purple" finish beautifully.


At the heart of the sound is the Nordstrand Bigman 5 pickup.

Often used as a Music Man-style bass pickup, this is a humbucker-type unit (featuring two single-coil coils wired in parallel).

It offers a wide, natural frequency range and fast attack, making it a recommended choice for players of all styles.

It also possesses the delicacy associated with Jazz Bass pickups, making it versatile enough to excel across any genre.


It is equipped with a Humpback Engineering 4117 preamp. While 2-band EQ systems often lean toward a "scooped" sound (emphasizing highs and lows), this instrument is designed to enhance the thickness of the mid-range even when the EQ is set flat. It adds a solid core and just the right amount of character to the original tone, creating a sound that cuts through the mix in a band setting.


Its versatility is further enhanced by the switching options.

It features two mini-switches:

One is a 3-way switch (Series/Coil-tap/Parallel), covering a wide range of tonal possibilities.

The other is an Active/Passive toggle switch—a truly ideal configuration.


The control layout is simple and intuitive, featuring a master volume and a 2-band EQ (Treble/Bass).


---------------------------------------------------------------

【A Special "Lorentz" Bonus!】


One final highlight... it comes with a hat!!!!!!!!!!

It's stylish and cute!!!!!!!!!

It looks warm and cozy!


We look forward to hearing from you!

---------------------------------------------------------------


SPEC


Model : Lorentz 5

Body Color : Sonic Blue Satin

Body Wood : Alder

Neck : Maple

Fretboard : Rosewood

Inlay: White Dot w/Brass ring

String Spacing : 18mm

Scale : 30.5"

Radius : 12"

Frets : 21

Nut : Cow Bone

Pickguard : Parchment 3-ply

Bridge : Nova Headless Bridge (5-string)

Pickup : Nordstrand Bigman 5

Preamp : Humpback Engineering 4117

Controls : Vol/Treble/Bass

Mini-SW1 (Series/Coil-tap/Parallel)

Mini-SW2 (Active/Passive)

Hardware Color : Black

Finish : Satin Finish

Serial Number : #881026141

Includes full accessory set, and gig case.

----------------------------------

item.title: 【new】Sire / V7 Vintage Ash-4 NT/HM New Generation 4.710kg #2N26550076【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,570
item.description:
$1,570 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sire became famous for releasing basses supervised by Marcus Miller. (Titles omitted)


The quality of their guitars and basses is extremely high, and they have gained strong support not only from Marcus Miller fans but also from a wider audience.


This is the latest model, the ""New Generation"" V7 Vintage Ash-4, modeled after a 70s JB bass.


While it's a replica of a 70s model, it incorporates modern features such as active circuitry, Luminlay construction, and lightweight hardware.


The body is made of ash.


The neck and fretboard are made of maple.


The fretboard edges are nicely rounded, aiming to improve playability.


Most players will likely have a positive impression of this feature.


The fretboard radius is 7.25 inches, faithfully reproducing the vintage style.


The pickups are the newly designed Marcus Vintage-J Revolution Pickup set.


It features a tight, trebly sound characteristic of a 70s JB bass.


This bass features a newly designed Marcus Vintage-J Revolution Pickup set.


It boasts a tight, trebly sound characteristic of 70s JB basses.


The preamp is a Marcus Heritage-3.


In the Vintage model, the mid-frequency range has been removed for more intuitive sound shaping.


The moderately scooped midrange, tight, low-end bass, and sharp, sparkling treble evoke Marcus Miller's playing style.


It is particularly well-suited for percussive slap techniques and 16th-note fingerstyle playing where attack is crucial.


This bass is a perfect match for those who love a classic look while wanting the practical balance of functionality from an active bass.


●SPECS

-BODY-


Body Material: Swamp Ash

Body Shape: Marcus Miller Jazz Shape (New Gen)

Body Color: NT (Natural)

Neck Material: Hard Maple

Neck Shape: C-Shape (New Gen)

Fingerboard Radius: 7.25""

Frets: 2.0 mm Small, 20 Frets

String Nut: Bone Nut, 38mm Width

Inlay: BK Block

Binding: BK 1ply Neck Joint: Four Bolt Steel Plate


-ELECTRONICS-

Preamp: Marcus Heritage-3 (No-Mid Frequency)

Controls: Volume / Tone, Blender, Treble / Middle, Bass (P / P for Passive (Mode)

Knobs: Plastic J-bass Type in Black (New Gen)


-HARDWARE-


Bridge: Marcus Miller Vintage-S

Tuning Gear: Premium Light Weight Open Gear

Hardware Finish: Chrome

Pick guard: BK 3ply


-CASE-


Gig Bag


Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

item.title: Sadowsky Guitars MetroLine 21-Fret Vintage J/J Bass 5st (SNB) [Ikebe 50th Anniversary Special Edition] #006085 — $4,424.91
item.description:
$4,424.91 · Brand New · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

We are receiving the customer order with first comes first serves basis both on website and at real shop.
So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

[Ikebe Musical Instrument Store 50th Anniversary Model]
This special edition features a sandblasted finish, with wood selected and ordered by Ikebe staff at a German factory. This is a 5-string model!

This special edition model was created as part of a 50th anniversary project, building on the long-standing relationship of trust between Sadowsky, Warwick (manufacturing/distribution factory), and Ikebe. Since the transition to German manufacturing, it is the world's first shop-ordered Metro Line (regular series) model.

Equipped with original parts such as a lightweight chambered body with an internal cavity, JJ pickups with hum-canceling structure, an active circuit with treble/bass boost EQ and bypass, passive tone control, ultra-lightweight tuners (tuners), and a bridge that produces excellent response, the guitar boasts a wide, flat, "resonant sound" that responds well to the touch of the pliers, and high playability, boasting world-class pro-spec sound that is directly connected to the stage, recording, and other live performances.

This special edition of the highly acclaimed German-made Metro Line features a sandblasted finish, a finish not normally available. By blasting fine particles into the wood, the surface is shaved, creating a three-dimensional effect on the ducts, which are then finished in a two-tone color that highlights the ducts. This custom specification was previously only available on a select few Warwick models, making it a rare model, with only a handful produced for the globally acclaimed Warwick Ikebe custom model. While achieving a unique look not found on standard models, the same quality remains true (in fact, perhaps due to the limited production, the instrument is crafted with a sense of commitment), allowing you to enjoy the unique Sadowsky sound.
Of course, Ikebe staff carefully selected each piece from the vast stock of wood at the local factory in Germany, taking into account factors such as weight, grain, and texture.

This model was made possible thanks to our long-standing strong relationship of trust with Sadowsky/Warwick and the special opportunity of Ikebe's 50th anniversary. Please come and experience it for yourself.

Machineheads Sadowsky Light
Nut Sadowsky Just-A-Nut III
Neck Wood: Maple
Fretboard Maple
Fingerboard Radius 12
Scale length 34 (long scale)
Width Nut 47.5 mm / 1.87
Frets 21 Nickel Silver (w: 2.3 mm / h: 1.2 mm)
Body Shape Sleek Undersized J
Body Wood Swamp Ash
Pickups Passive Sadowsky J-Style Hum Canceling (60's Bridge Position)
Electronics Active Sadowsky 2-Way Electronics + Vintage Tone Control
Pot layout Volume / Balance / Vintage Tone Control (P/P) / Treble and Bass (stacked)
Bridge system Sadowsky Bridge w/ Quick Release
Strap system S-Style Security Locks
Construction Bolt-On
Hardware Chrome
Strings Sadowsky Stainless Steel 045-135
Sandblasted Nirvana Black/Silver Grain Trans. Satin
Weight ≈ 3.72 kg

Gig bag included

“MOON GUITARS,” a world-renowned, long-established manufacturer founded by some of Japan’s leading luthiers,

is favored by renowned artists such as Larry Graham, John Mayer, Mitsufune Okamine, and Mitsuru Sudo.


Its high versatility—making it suitable for any genre—is one of the reasons it is loved by so many artists, and it speaks to the instrument’s exceptional quality.



The model we’re introducing today is a passive bass custom-ordered by our store.


It features a classic combination of a carefully selected two-piece alder body, a maple neck, and a rosewood fingerboard.

Essentially a basic passive bass with the vibe of a ’60s jazz bass, it delivers superb playability and a rich tone thanks to craftsmanship honed over many years.


For the fingerboard, we’ve chosen Honduran rosewood—a variety known for imparting a tight, focused tone.

It’s characterized by its high density and a sound with real substance.


Additionally, while Moon typically features an ultra-thin lacquer finish as standard, this model features a urethane finish.

The added thickness results in a tighter, fuller sound.


It’s equipped with Moon’s original Oxalis JB-classic pickup.

While rooted in a vintage style, the sound offers modern playability.

With a solid, substantial feel, the low end is tight yet sufficiently full-bodied, while the high end offers a pleasant clarity. Particularly in an ensemble setting, the notes stand out clearly—never getting lost in the mix—and the guitar makes a strong presence.


This is a guitar that brings out every nuance of the player’s expression, regardless of genre.


Please take this opportunity to try it out!


[SPEC]

Body: 2-Piece Alder

Neck: Maple

Fingerboard: Honduran Rosewood

Pickups: Moon Oxalis JB-4

Parts: Chrome

Finish: Urethane

Controls: 2 Volume, 1 Tone

item.title: Eko Kadett P07 Reissue - 4-String Bass - Made in Italy - 90s - Tobacco Sunburst - Vintage-style Precision Bass — $349
item.description:
$349 · Fair · Eccentric Guitars

Eko is an Italian Guitar manufacturer that dates all the way back to the late 50s. They've produced numerous original designs as well as an equal amount of copies of other models, with some calling them the Teisco or Fujigen of Italy.

The Kadett P07 is one of their original designs, and from the little information I could gather online they're seemingly reissues of a 70s model and were produced in small numbers between the 90s to mid-2000s.

The overall condition of this one visually is very good but there are a few issues with the bass. Most notably the tone control doesn't function and either needs replaced or possibly just resoldering. The G-string tuner rattles acoustically when plucking hard, and lastly there are faint outlines along the fretboard where someone previously applied block inlay stickers. It's otherwise very clean and has a great and vibrant look to it with minimal wear overall.

Playability-wise the bass is very good as well. It has low action at around 2.0mm at the 12th fret, a straight neck and hardware that functions perfectly, including the truss rod which has plenty of room left in it. It also weighs in at a solid 8.9Ibs.

My favorite aspect of the bass is the tone. It has a very clean and meaty classic P-bass sound which slides perfectly in the mix yet has a surprising amount of punch and clarity to it when you slap it.

item.title: Dingwall SP1 5-String Vintage Burst Gloss — $2,659.35
item.description:
$2,659.35 · Brand New · Dijkmans Guitars

De Dingwall SP1 die bewijst dat fanned frets en klassiek P-bass DNA perfect samengaan

De Dingwall SP1 is geen compromis tussen traditie en innovatie — het is een instrument dat beide werelden volledig omarmt. Geïnspireerd op de Custom Shop Super P, maar ontworpen voor een breder publiek, combineert deze vijfsnarige bas de vertrouwde ergonomie van Leo Fenders iconische blauwdruk met de kenmerkende Dingwall multi-scale toets. Het resultaat is een instrument dat meteen vertrouwd aanvoelt, maar je speelbeleving naar een hoger niveau tilt.

De multi-scale constructie met een schaallengte van 32" tot 35" zorgt voor een uitzonderlijk strakke en gedefinieerde lage B-snaar, terwijl de hogere snaren soepel en responsief blijven. De Medium Thin C-hals van één stuk esdoorn ligt comfortabel in de hand, en het rozenhout fretboard met dots-inlays geeft de SP1 een warme, klassieke uitstraling. De combinatie van een elzenhouten body en de Dingwall Dual-P pickup aan de hals met de FD3n aan de brug levert een breed tonaal palet: van diepe, volle P-bass warmte tot heldere, articulerende bridgetonen. De 4-positie Quad-Tone pickup selector maakt het eenvoudig om snel te schakelen tussen karakters, aangevuld met een master volume en master tone voor fijnafstemming.

De SP1 wordt geleverd met een softcase en is afgewerkt in een prachtige Vintage Burst gloss lak. Voor de bassist die op zoek is naar een veelzijdig, goed gebouwd instrument met de Dingwall-signatuur, is dit een kans die je niet voorbij laat gaan.

item.title: 【new】Sadowsky / MX21 VJ4 CAO/M 3.680kg #J000309-25【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,363
item.description:
$1,363 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top-tier musicians like Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients.

Previously, the Metroline Express was manufactured in Japan, but as Warwick took over distribution of Sadowsky products, manufacturing shifted to a factory in China.

While keeping the selling price down, high quality is maintained, making it a very unique product in its price range.

Initially, the Metroline Express series didn't have the Sadowsky logo, but it has since been successfully changed to the current one.

Furthermore, the neck has been changed to roasted maple.

It has a satin finish and a smooth feel.

The back panel is screwless, similar to Warwick guitars.

Looking inside the cavity, you can see that it's shielded with copper foil tape. It's excellent.

The pickups are hum-canceling, as is typical of Sadowsky.

The preamp circuit board is almost identical to that of the top-of-the-line models priced around 1 million yen, which is a significant advantage.

The controls are volume, balance, bass, and treble.

A push-pull switch allows you to bypass the active circuit and play passively.

The powerful and crisp sound sounds a little more flamboyant than recent NYC models.

Some people might even prefer this sound based on the tone alone.

This is a guitar with extremely high potential.

It seems unlikely that a product of the same high quality in the same price range would be easy to produce.

●SPECS

4-String Electric Bass
Scale Length: 34"" (864 mm)
Bolt-On Construction
Body Wood: Okoume
Neck Wood: Roasted Maple
Fingerboard: Morado (Pau Ferro)
Fingerboard Radius: 12""
Nut: Sadowsky Just-A-Nut III
Nut Width: 1.54" (39.2 mm)
Frets: 21 Nickel Silver (width: 0.094"" (2.4 mm) / height: 0.051"" (1.3 mm))
Neck Pickup: Passive hum-cancelling Sadowsky J-Style
Bridge Pickup: Passive hum-cancelling Sadowsky J-Style in 60s position
Electronics: Sadowsky Active 2-Way
Controls: Volume / Balance / Treble / Bass
Push/pull function at Bass knob for Preamp Bypass
Tuners: Sadowsky Light machine heads with open gear
Security Locks: Sadowsky S-style
Bridge System: Sadowsky bridge with quick string release
Hardware Color: Chrome
Strings: 045 - 105 Stainless Steel
Included Accessories: Sadowsky Fretboard Protector and Hex Key Tools
Gig Bag: Sadowsky Portabag (SAC BAG BASS PORTA)

Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: Genuine gig bag, wrench, warranty card

Text: Tomoki Hayakawa

Inspection 담당: Kubota

Product Photos: Tei

Manufacturer's warranty included

●Lifetime free maintenance

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free "string vibration setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

・Please present your warranty card with your name filled in.

*This offer is only valid for customers who purchased the product at a GIB or TONIQ physical store or online shop.

*Please note that shipping and other related expenses will be your responsibility.

*Repurchase Price Guarantee

If you sell your purchased item within 6 months of purchase, we guarantee the repurchase price. The price is 50% for new items and 70% for used items.

*Defect Warranty

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. Excluding some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.

The warranty period is 3 months for used items and 1 year for new items.

Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:

*Malfunctions and damage caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.

*Replacement of consumables.

*Malfunctions and damage caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.

*Malfunctions and damage caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual. * If parts repaired or modified by a party other than our company malfunction or are damaged.

* If the warranty certificate does not have your name, model name, serial number, etc. filled in, or if the wording has been altered.

* If the warranty certificate is not presented or is lost.

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan.)

● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in

When you purchase a new instrument, you will receive a 10% discount on the sales price when you use our trade-in service. (Consignment items are excluded. This discount applies only if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the sales price.)

Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.

We ship with sufficient packaging.

We also ship internationally.

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: G&L LB-100 Tribute 2020 Olympic White — $589.51
item.description:
$589.51 · Very Good · bassNguitar

G&L, after Fender and Music Man, is the third brand founded by Leo Fender and his partner George Fullerton. This LB100 clearly displays the features of the Precision Bass. The maple neck boasts a rosewood fingerboard with a 9.5-inch radius, and the scale length is 34 inches. This is an affordable Tribute model, produced in Indonesia, offering excellent value for money.


More pix and details upon request.

Shipping worldwide available.

item.title: Knooren 5 custom — $2,858.22
item.description:
$2,858.22 · Excellent · GAVRIIL KAMPANTAIS

For sale this unique custom built 5 string bass made by Jan Knooren from the Netherlands. Jan built basses for John Patitucci, Brian Bromberg (own signature) and many more famous artists (mostly US and Japan). It features a cherry body, flamed maple neck, rosewood fingerboard, EMG preamp and very good sounding Lane Poor pickups. You don't see them very often anymore. The bass weighs 4.3kg and balances perfectly. Very warm and punchy sounding bass with a strong B string.

item.title: 【new】Sire / V7 Vintage Alder-5 NT/HM New Generation 4.250kg #2N26330014【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,554
item.description:
$1,554 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sire became famous for releasing basses supervised by Marcus Miller. (Titles omitted)


The quality of their guitars and basses is extremely high, and they have gained strong support not only from Marcus Miller fans but also from a wider audience.


This is the latest model, the ""New Generation"" V7 Vintage Alder-5, modeled after a 1970s JB bass.


While it's a replica of a 1970s model, it incorporates modern features such as active circuitry, Luminlay construction, and lightweight hardware.


The body is made of North American Alder.


The neck is 34-inch scale Hard Maple, and the fingerboard is also made of Hard Maple.


The fingerboard features an edgeless finish with rounded edges, making it suitable for players who prefer smooth fingering.


Most players will likely have a positive impression of this feature.


The fingerboard radius is 7.25 inches, faithfully reproducing the vintage style.


This bass features a newly designed Marcus Vintage-J Revolution Pickup set.


It boasts a tight, trebly sound characteristic of 70s JB basses.


The preamp is a Marcus Heritage-3.


In the Vintage model, the mid-frequency range has been removed for more intuitive sound shaping.


The moderately scooped midrange, tight, low-end bass, and sharp, sparkling treble evoke Marcus Miller's playing style.


It is particularly well-suited for percussive slap techniques and 16th-note fingerstyle playing where attack is crucial.


This bass is a perfect match for those who love a classic look while wanting the wide range of a 5-string bass and the functionality of an active bass, all in a practical and balanced way.


⚪︎SPECS


-BODY-


Body Material: North American Alder

Body Shape: Marcus Miller Jazz Shape (New Gen)

Body Color: NT (Natural), TS (Tobacco Sunburst), TP (Tide Pool)


-NECK-


Neck Material: Hard Maple (NT, TS) and Roasted Hard Maple (TP)

Neck Shape: C-Shape (New Gen)

Scale: 34""

Fingerboard Material: Hard Maple (NT), Ebony (TS) and Roasted Hard Maple (TP) with Edgeless™ (Rolled Fretboard Edges)

Fingerboard Radius: 7.25""

Frets: 2.0 mm Small, 20 Frets

String Nut: Bone Nut, 46mm Width

Inlay: BK Dot (NT) and Clay Dot (TS, TP)

Binding: BK 1ply (NT) and IV 1ply (TS, TP)

Neck Joint: Four Bolt Steel Plate


-ELECTRONICS-


Pickup: Marcus Vintage-J Revolution Pickup set

Preamp: Marcus Heritage-3 (No-Mid Frequency)

Controls: Volume / Tone, Blender, Treble / Middle, Bass (P / P for Passive mode)

Knobs: Plastic J-bass Type in Black (New Gen)


-HARDWARE-


Bridge: Marcus Miller Vintage-S

Tuning Gear: Premium Light Weight Open Gear

Hardware Finish: Chrome

Pickguard: Tortoise


-CASE-


Gig Bag


Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.


Accessories: Original gig bag, wrench, warranty card, instruction manual, pickup fence

• Frets/Inlays: 24 frets with black dot inlays
• Nut width: 45 mm
• Headstock: matching headstock
• Construction: bolt on
• Scale length: long scale 34"
• Pickguard: transparent
• Pickups: Delano JC 5 AL set with1 mini switch (serial/single coil/parallel) and 3-way switch (400Hz/600Hz/800Hz)
• Electronics: active 3-band Maruszczyk Toneformer 3MS/TP- volume (active/passive push/pull switch), balance, bass, middle, treble/passive tone blend
• Finish: turquoise, transparent, body gloss/neck matte
• Hardware: chrome, Monorail-bridge with 19 mm string spacing, Dome-style knobs, Standard Y-tuner

Weight: 3,91 kg
incl. Gigbag

item.title: Sire M2 4-string v2 2023 - Black — $550
item.description:
$550 · Very Good · Chloe's Cool Stuff

This M2 has undergone a huge hug since I acquired it. I got it new from Thomann's. Here's a list of the work that was performed on this. All upgrades/work is free, as expected with any "mods" to an instrument.

  • Over time I discovered the body was made of a very squishy wood. I sealed it in a treatment that has preserve the body rather well. It's been buffed to a sheen gloss and is resistant to major wear.
  • The headstock and cutaway at the base were polished to a shine.
  • The neck was treated, sanded to a fine polish and is highly resistant to major wear. This is a oil/wax treatment and not a lacquer or traditional method.
  • GOTOH lightweight tuners.
  • The bass showed up with clips holding the wiring in place. I soldered everything in place and added full shielding in the cavity. I also added two switches to it for a total of three (1 - coil split, 2 - series/parallel, 3 - active passive). The knob layout is still the same and all works correctly (be aware that the blend knob needs to be centered when in series, otherwise the bass completely goes dead and appears broken). Switchcraft input jack.
  • Replaced the stock saddles with Hipshot saddles.
  • Dunlop strap lock button already installed.
  • Two fresh Energizer batteries.
  • New GHS brite flats recently installed.
  • Headstock tuner and fret wrap included.
  • There is a smudge on the back near the battery box. Something weird happened in that one spot during the treatment process.
